symbols: figure resting against a haystack — the laborer at rest, the body's need for respite from toil, echoing the divine pattern of work and rest
large round haystack — the gathered harvest, the fruit of labor, the provision of daily bread
blue sky above open fields — the expansiveness of creation under which human labor takes place
hat pulled over the face — deep sleep, surrender, the vulnerability of rest
hands folded on the lap — work temporarily ceased, peace in stillness
description: A painting after Millet's composition showing a farm worker resting at midday, leaning against a large round haystack in an open agricultural landscape. The figure sits on the ground with legs extended, wearing working clothes — a green jacket, blue shirt, and brown boots — with a cap pulled low over the face in sleep. Hands rest folded on the lap. The enormous golden-brown haystack dominates the right side of the composition. Behind stretches a flat landscape of green and brown fields under a vivid blue sky.
